_notes: Country-level import dependency data. Used to compute second-order impacts when commodity flows are disrupted. Sources: IEA, UN Comtrade, World Bank, FAO. Values are approximate pre-crisis baselines.

_fields.oil_import_pct: fraction of oil consumption that is imported (1.0 = fully import-dependent)
_fields.gulf_oil_fraction: of oil imports, fraction from Gulf states
_fields.lng_from_gulf: fraction of natural gas/LNG imports from Gulf
_fields.urea_from_gulf: fraction of nitrogen fertilizer imports from Gulf region
_fields.food_import_pct: fraction of food calories that are imported
_fields.spr_days: strategic petroleum reserve days of import coverage
_fields.helium_from_qatar: fraction of industrial helium from Qatar (if significant)
_fields.vulnerability_score: composite 0-1 score, higher = more exposed to Gulf disruption

countries.name: Japan
countries.notes: IEA member with strong strategic reserves but extreme Gulf crude dependency. LNG imports diverse (Australia, Malaysia, Russia-Sakhalin, US LNG). Agriculture import dependent.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.notes: Extreme semiconductor exposure via helium dependency. Major chipmakers Samsung and SK Hynix at risk from helium shortage. High Gulf oil dependency.

countries.name: China
countries.notes: World's largest oil importer. Gulf dependency significant but diversified (Russia, West Africa, Americas). World's largest urea producer — low fertilizer import dependency. Large SPR.

countries.name: India
countries.notes: Critically low strategic reserves (14 days). High Gulf dependency for both oil and fertilizer feedstock. India is world's second largest urea consumer and depends on Gulf gas for domestic production. Food largely self-sufficient but fertilizer input dependency is severe.

countries.name: Bangladesh
countries.notes: Extremely high vulnerability. Minimal reserves, very high Gulf dependency for oil and fertilizer. Domestic fertilizer plants depend on imported LNG. Critical food import dependency. One of the most exposed countries globally.

countries.name: Pakistan
countries.notes: High vulnerability compounded by existing economic fragility. Domestic fertilizer plants depend on imported LNG. Food security already strained.

countries.name: Indonesia
countries.notes: Partial domestic oil production buffers exposure. LNG exporter (Bontang). Moderate vulnerability.

countries.name: Thailand
countries.notes: Food exporter (rice) but oil-dependent. Agricultural productivity at risk via fertilizer channel.

countries.name: Singapore
countries.notes: Major refining and trading hub. Highly exposed but has strategic reserves and market access to reroute.

countries.name: Germany
countries.notes: Diversified oil sourcing (North Sea, Americas, North Africa). IEA 90-day reserves. Global oil price increase is primary exposure.

countries.name: Italy
countries.notes: Higher Gulf exposure than North European peers. Kuwait Petroleum co-owns Milazzo refinery.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.notes: North Sea production partially offsets imports. High food import dependency is secondary risk.

countries.name: United States
countries.notes: Domestic production (shale) dramatically reduced oil import dependency. Massive SPR (~600 Mbbl). Primary exposure is global price effects from market disruption, not direct supply. Helium domestic production buffers tech sector but cannot replace Qatar volumes.

countries.name: Egypt
countries.notes: Critical wheat importer — one of world's largest. Food import dependency via Black Sea/US/France. Oil partially domestic. Suez Canal revenues affected by rerouting.

countries.name: Turkey
countries.notes: Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline terminates in Turkey — provides some strategic leverage. Moderate Gulf dependency.

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.notes: Oil exporter but imports refined products. Fertilizer dependency via global price effects. Food import exposure. Refinery capacity insufficient domestically.

countries.name: Brazil
countries.notes: Large domestic oil production (Petrobras). Major agricultural exporter but highly fertilizer-import dependent. Brazil imports 85%+ of its fertilizers — global price shock significant.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.notes: Primary Gulf exporter. Has East-West Pipeline bypass to Red Sea but still transit-dependent for ~70% of exports. Saudi Arabia itself is impacted by revenue loss.

countries.name: Qatar
countries.notes: World's largest LNG exporter. No bypass for LNG. Ras Laffan damaged. Revenue loss severe.
